task_completion.description: Load the SRTM DEM file into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'srtm_dem.tif' is visible in the Product Explorer, layer manager, or file open dialog of the GIS application (e.g., SNAP).

task_completion.description: Open the elevation band to view the DEM data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image view of the DEM data is open in the main workspace, showing the terrain (initially likely in grayscale).

task_completion.description: Apply a non-grayscale graduated terrain/topography color palette to the elevation band.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Color Manipulation tool or layer styling panel is open, and the DEM image view displays a colored terrain palette (e.g., a gradient of greens, yellows, browns, and whites) instead of grayscale.

task_completion.description: Save the processed product in BEAM-DIMAP format to the specified export path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Product' dialog or file manager shows the file being saved as 'dem_colored.dim' in the '/home/ga/snap_exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the colored visualization as a Google Earth KMZ file to the specified export path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' dialog (e.g., Export -> View as Google Earth KMZ) or file manager shows the file being saved as 'dem_terrain.kmz' in the '/home/ga/snap_exports/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS software's graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SNAP (or similar GIS) GUI menus, toolbars, and dialogs, rather than just terminal commands executing scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied a color palette within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Color Manipulation window or layer properties dialog is visible showing the color mapping applied to the elevation band, proving the color was generated in-app.

integrity.description: The agent navigated to or typed the exact export paths in the software's save/export dialogs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save/export dialog boxes show the correct directories and filenames being entered, rather than using a terminal 'mv' or 'cp' command to fake the output files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/snap_data/srtm_dem.tif. The expected BEAM-DIMAP export path is /home/ga/snap_exports/dem_colored.dim. The expected Google Earth KMZ export path is /home/ga/snap_exports/dem_terrain.kmz. The color palette applied must be a non-grayscale graduated terrain/topography palette.
